name: Jaylen Washington
chief_complaint: I have a killer headache, my neck is super stiff, the light hurts my eyes, and I feel really hot. I've been throwing up all morning.
onset_description: Progressive headache, fever, and neck stiffness developing over the past 18 hours. Started as a mild headache yesterday afternoon. Woke this morning with severe headache, high fever, and vomiting. Roommate brought him to the ED when he seemed confused and couldn't tolerate light.

headache.description: My head is pounding, it's like the worst headache I've ever had, it just keeps getting worse
headache.onset: 18 hours ago, started mild, progressively worsening
headache.radiation: down the back of the neck
alleviating_factors: nothing - took Tylenol and Advil with no relief

fever.description: I've been burning up since last night, soaking through my sheets with sweat
alleviating_factors: Tylenol brought it down slightly but it came right back

neck_stiffness.description: I can't bend my neck forward at all, it's completely locked up and it makes the headache way worse if I try
neck_stiffness.onset: noticed this morning, about 8 hours ago

photophobia.description: The lights are killing me, I can't open my eyes all the way, it's way too bright in here

vomiting.description: I've thrown up like four or five times since this morning, can't keep anything down

altered_mental_status.description: Roommate reports: 'He was saying weird stuff this morning, didn't know what day it was, seemed out of it.' Patient is oriented to person and place but not date. Intermittently confused.

rash.description: Roommate: 'I noticed some reddish-purple spots on his legs and stomach.' Patient has scattered petechiae on lower extremities and trunk.
rash.onset: noticed this morning but may have been developing overnight

symptoms_absent: sore throat (initially had mild one yesterday, now overshadowed by other symptoms)

labs.CBC: WBC 22.5 (markedly elevated with 90% neutrophils and 8% bands - left shift), Hgb 14.8, Plt 125 (low - early DIC concern)
labs.BMP: Na 133 (low), K 4.0, Cr 1.3 (mildly elevated - dehydration), Glucose 92, BUN 24
labs.blood_cultures: Drawn x2 from separate sites, pending
labs.CSF_analysis: Pending LP - expected findings for bacterial meningitis: elevated WBC (neutrophil predominant), elevated protein, low glucose, positive Gram stain

past_medical_history: Concussion from basketball (2 years ago, fully recovered)
medications: Albuterol inhaler as needed (hasn't used in over a year)
Acetaminophen 500mg taken x4 in last 24 hours
Ibuprofen 400mg taken x2 in last 24 hours

social_history.alcohol: Drinks on weekends with friends, maybe 5-6 drinks when going out
social_history.occupation: College sophomore, biology major, lives in dormitory
social_history.living_situation: Lives in a double dorm room on campus. Shares bathroom with the floor. Roommate is present and providing history.
social_history.exercise: Plays intramural basketball 3 times per week, works out at campus gym
social_history.diet: Campus dining hall, protein shakes, typical college diet

personality: normally outgoing and confident but currently very ill and frightened. Compliant with care. His roommate is more anxious than he is and keeps asking if he'll be okay.
communication_style: When lucid, speaks in short sentences due to pain. Uses young adult language: 'this is the worst, bro' and 'I feel like I'm dying.' Intermittently confused and may give inconsistent answers. Roommate provides supplemental history. Becomes more cooperative when addressed calmly.
pain_description_style: Says 'my head is about to explode' and rates it 9/10. Keeps eyes squeezed shut. Holds his head in his hands. Moans with movement. During confused periods, may not reliably rate pain.

critical_findings: Classic meningitis triad: fever (103.4F), neck stiffness (nuchal rigidity), altered mental status
Non-blanching petechial rash - concerning for meningococcemia (Neisseria meningitidis)
Markedly elevated WBC (22.5) with bandemia (left shift) indicating severe bacterial infection
Elevated procalcitonin (8.2) strongly suggesting bacterial etiology
Elevated lactate (3.2) suggesting early sepsis/tissue hypoperfusion
Tachycardia (118) and relative hypotension (104/62) - early sepsis presentation
Thrombocytopenia (Plt 125) and prolonged INR - early DIC concern
College dormitory living - high-risk setting for meningococcal transmission
Progressive deterioration over 18 hours with confusion developing
rubric.diagnosis: Bacterial Meningitis (suspected Neisseria meningitidis given petechial rash and demographics)
differential_diagnoses: Viral meningitis (less likely given severity and labs)
recommended_interventions: DO NOT delay antibiotics for lumbar puncture - start empiric antibiotics IMMEDIATELY
IV ceftriaxone 2g plus IV vancomycin (empiric coverage)
IV dexamethasone 0.15mg/kg before or with first dose of antibiotics (reduces complications)
Lumbar puncture as soon as feasible (send CSF for cell count, protein, glucose, Gram stain, culture, meningitis PCR panel)
Blood cultures x2 from separate sites
Aggressive IV fluid resuscitation (sepsis protocol - 30mL/kg crystalloid bolus)
Droplet isolation precautions until meningococcal disease excluded
Close contacts (roommate) need chemoprophylaxis (rifampin or ciprofloxacin)
Monitor for signs of increased intracranial pressure, DIC, adrenal crisis (Waterhouse-Friderichsen syndrome)
